core_model.structure_summary: A continuous-time relaxation of a state vector following the negative gradient of a scalar energy functional, whose fixed point equals a three-stage map that scores a query against stored rows by a similarity, passes the scores through a nonlinear separation (softmax-type) function, and projects back through the stored matrix.
core_model.canonical_form: z = P\,\mathrm{sep}(\mathrm{sim}(M,q)); \qquad \tau\,\dot v = -\partial E/\partial v, \quad E(v) = \tfrac12\sum_i v_i^2 - \log\sum_j e^{\mathrm{sim}(M_{ij},v_i)}; \qquad v^{t+1} = M\,\sigma(M^{\top} v^t)

variables.meaning: vector being iteratively relaxed toward a stored pattern
relaxation parameter for the gradient dynamics
stored matrix of memory rows against which the state is scored and reprojected
Lyapunov energy functional whose descent drives the dynamics

notes: The unifying object is the similarity-separation-projection map, realized equivalently as gradient descent on a Lyapunov energy with second-order (pairwise) neuron interactions; softmax separation gives the log-sum-exp energy. Could also be tagged discrete_update given the iterated fixed-point map z=P sigma(M q).
